packageRules.description: npm: group minor/patch; majors stay individual
npm: pre-1.0 packages treat 0.x→0.y as breaking (semver convention, ^0.x.y resolves like ~0.x.y); ungroup from minor/patch so they land as individual PRs

Node majors: block automatic bumps across every surface — .nvmrc (nvm), engines.node and @types/node (npm), actions/setup-node node-version (github-actions). Node majors are upgraded manually and only to even LTS releases (e.g. 24 → 26).

@tsconfig/nodeXX: the Node major version is encoded in the package name (@tsconfig/node22 → @tsconfig/node24 means switching Node 22 → 24). Renovate proposes those as 'replacement' updates; block them alongside the rest of the Node-major lockdown.
typescript: no automatic major bumps. TS majors cascade to typescript-eslint, plugin matrices, and consumers of Hardhat's TS-aware APIs — bump manually when the ecosystem catches up.
@nomicfoundation/edr: tightly coupled to Hardhat releases and rides a -next.N prerelease cadence. Bumped manually alongside Hardhat changesets, not on Renovate's schedule.
vite + @vitejs/plugin-react: group together — plugin-react has a peer-dependency on vite, so landing one without the other breaks the build.
